sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: Oh, hey Abel. Heard you survived your first day.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: Small town. Word doesn't have far to travel.
Jeff smiled, but it didn't quite reach his eyes.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: So what do you do around here, Jeff? Besides getting people settled in.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: I teach. At the school, up the hill on the east side. Been there for... well, a long time.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: A schoolteacher. In a town this small?
He said this with a practiced ease that suggested he'd answered the question many times before.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: Anyway, you should get some rest. The bay's a different animal than the lagoon, from what I hear.
